source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Ansistrano Deployments: Limiting releases, shared paths/files, repo subtree, git branch/tag

questions.q: Which variable limits how many releases are kept?
questions.why: ansistrano_keep_releases: N keeps the N most recent releases and prunes the rest.

questions.q: What do ansistrano_shared_paths and ansistrano_shared_files do?
options: Define folders/files symlinked from shared/ into every release so they persist
questions.why: They symlink content (e.g. css, img, logs) from each release to shared/, so it survives across releases.

questions.q: The repo has an html/ subfolder holding the site. Which variable deploys just that subdirectory?
questions.why: ansistrano_git_repo_tree names the sub-directory of the repo to deploy, avoiding the extra level.

questions.q: Which variable deploys a specific git branch or tag?
questions.why: ansistrano_git_branch takes a branch (e.g. releases/v1.1.0) or a tag (e.g. v2.0.0).

questions.q: When you set ansistrano_git_repo_tree: 'html', what else must change on the server?
questions.why: Stripping the html level means the vhost documentroot changes from {{ dest }}current/html to {{ dest }}current/.
